content.text:
Use `--bench` (with the dash dash) instead of passing the name as a positional filter:

```
cargo bench --bench goalzc-crit
```

What you ran (`cargo bench goalzc-crit`) treats `goalzc-crit` as a *filter* passed into every bench/test binary cargo walks — so cargo still compiles and invokes every target, and each one prints its `running 0 tests` summary as it filters everything out. `--bench <name>` selects one bench target up front and skips the rest entirely.

If you want to be even tighter:

```
cargo bench --bench goalzc-crit -- --quiet           # suppresses criterion's per-bench banner
cargo bench -p actor-x1 --bench goalzc-crit          # workspaces with bench-name collisions
```

A couple of side notes:

- `cargo bench --quiet` (before the `--`) doesn't help here — the noisy lines are libtest's own output, not cargo's.
- The same trick works for tests: `cargo test --test <name>` vs `cargo test <filter>`.

content.text:
Apples-to-apples: `goalzc 10 -w 3` → **0.281 M msg/s**. goalzc-crit median ≈ 232 K msg/s. So the real ratio is goalzc-crit / goalzc ≈ 0.825 — a ~17.5% gap, **wider** than my earlier 0.87 estimate, not narrower.

The bot thinks the structural reason is now clearer: in goalzc-crit, every criterion sample is a fresh `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` cycle (spawn threads → 50 ms warmup → 100 ms measure → join). The CPU never reaches the warm steady state that `goalzc 10 -w 3` settles into across one set of long-lived threads. Per-sample 50 ms warmup just isn't enough to overcome cold-spawn cache / frequency state.

input.new_string:
### Smoke results (`--size 64`, unpinned)

Apples-to-apples comparison: criterion's defaults give the
bench ~3 s warmup + 10 s measurement, so match it on the bin
side with `goalzc 10 -w 3`.

- `goalzc 10 -w 3 --size 64`: 0.281 M msg/s (steady state,
  long-lived threads).
- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: median ~232 K msg/s, criterion CI
  [222, 246] K elem/s.
- Ratio goalzc-crit / goalzc ≈ 0.825 — wider than the
  goal2 / goal2-crit reference (~0.95).
- The bot thinks the gap is structural to `iter_custom`: each
  criterion sample is a fresh `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` cycle
  (spawn threads → 50 ms warmup → 100 ms measure → join), so
  the CPU never reaches the warm cache / frequency state that
  `goalzc`'s single set of long-lived threads settles into.
  The 50 ms per-sample warmup just isn't enough to overcome
  cold-spawn state. Not a probe-contamination issue (the
  bench uses `run_no_probe`); it's the cost of criterion's
  "fresh runtime per sample" pattern.
- Future tweak: extend per-sample warmup (e.g. 200 ms) and
  reduce `sample_size` to keep total bench time bounded;
  trade narrower gap for fewer samples.

message.content: So goalzc-crit isn't testing the same thing as goalzc, I suggest we refactor the runtime so it has a trait with "startup", "run" and "teardown". ActorManager will use those and that sould make it easier for crit and our bench to measure the same thing. Thoughts no code.

content.text:
Diagnosis is right. Before committing to the refactor, a few thoughts:

**The structural fix you're describing is sound**

Splitting into `startup` / `run` / `teardown` means criterion's `iter_custom` calls only `run` against threads that are already warm — same shape goalzc has after its 3 s warmup. The criterion warmup phase (3 s of `run` calls before measurement begins) becomes useful warmup instead of being thrown away on each sample.

**Real questions before code**

- **How does `run` report counts?** Today, counts accumulate locally per-thread and surface at join. If `run` is sample-able mid-flight, we need either (a) per-actor `Arc<AtomicU64>` incremented on each dispatch — small `Relaxed` add, ~negligible at goal2/goalzc's µs scale, real at goal1's 5 ns scale; or (b) a `QueryCount` signal-and-reply round-trip — slower per `run` but no hot-path cost.
- **Probe vs no-probe split.** Today they're two separate methods. With lifecycle split, the probe choice is fixed at spawn, so it lands on `startup` — `startup_probe(...)` vs `startup_no_probe(...)`, or one fn with a flag.
- **Misuse safety.** Splitting opens "forgot teardown" / "ran before startup" failure modes. Mitigations: a `Handle` with `Drop` that shuts down if forgotten, or a typed-state pattern (`Idle → Running → Done`). Worth the cost if `RuntimeZC` is going to grow more lifecycle-aware over time.
- **Trait or just methods?** I'd defer the trait. Today there's exactly one runtime; "trait with three methods" is hypothetical-future design. Methods on `RuntimeZC` give us the same composability and we promote to a trait the day a second impl appears.

**Cheaper experiment worth running first**

Before refactoring, bump the bench's per-sample warmup from 50 ms → 500 ms (or 1 s) and drop `sample_size` from 50 → 10 to keep total bench time bounded. Same wall clock; each sample actually reaches steady state. If that closes most of the 17.5% gap, the structural cost of fresh-spawn-per-sample isn't the dominant factor and the refactor's payoff is smaller. If the gap stays wide, that's the empirical case for the lifecycle split.

The bot's read: the lifecycle refactor is worth doing eventually regardless — it's a cleaner API and future-proofs against M:N runtimes — but it's also bigger than goalzc-crit needs. The cheap experiment tells us which 0.5.0 sub-step it should land in (or whether it earns its own 0.6.0 ladder).

message.content: I'm not concerned with benchmarks I want to get the right API and this is just part of the journey, our current code isn't any different from goal2, the one difference is zero size hence no real messages. So we've already "tested" this runtime style and I want to move forward. Make sense, no code

content.text:
Makes sense. RuntimeZC is goal2 with `&[u8]` payload view — same threads, same mpsc, same orchestration. The runtime style was validated when goal2 landed; goalzc demonstrated the pool-backed payload variant works. Re-running cross-checks won't teach us anything new.

**What I'd do with 0.5.0-4 / 0.5.0**

Land 0.5.0-4 as-is (with the chores trimmed — drop the gap-anxiety paragraphs, keep one line noting the bench is a smoke test for the pool path, not a fidelity reference). Then close 0.5.0. Two reasons:
- The bench is cheap regression insurance against a future refactor — *does it still spin up, send, and tear down?* — even if it's not measuring what goalzc measures.
- A clean ladder close gives the lifecycle refactor a fresh `0.6.0-0` plan marker to start from. Tangling the refactor into 0.5.0 muddies the history.

**Then 0.6.0 — lifecycle refactor**

Plan-marker shape worth fleshing out before code:
- Decide methods-on-`RuntimeZC` vs trait. Recommendation: methods now, trait when a second runtime exists.
- Decide count-sampling: atomic counter vs signal-and-reply. Recommendation: `Arc<AtomicU64>` per actor — simpler API, hot-path cost is negligible at the cross-thread scale we're working at.
- Decide handle safety: bare `Handle` vs `Drop`-shutdown vs typed state. Recommendation: `Handle` with `Drop`-shutdown; teardown becomes "consume the Handle for results", and a forgotten teardown panics-on-drop or shuts down silently — pick one.
- Probe vs no-probe choice moves to `startup`.
- The bench naturally collapses: `setup` builds + warms a runtime once, `iter_custom` calls only `run(short_window)`, `teardown` at end. The 17.5% gap closes as a side effect of the right API, not as a goal.

Sound about right? If so, my next action is trimming the chores writeup, then asking again to push 0.5.0-4.
Trimming the chores writeup now.

file.content:
## benches/goalzc-crit (0.5.0-4)

Adds `goalzc-crit`, the criterion cross-validation bench for
`goalzc`. Drives the same two-thread two-actor zerocopy
ping-pong workload via `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` so the
measurement is probe-clean. Mirrors `goal2-crit`'s shape
(`iter_custom`, fresh runtime per sample, scale to iters).

### File-by-file

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-3` → `0.5.0-4`; new
  `[[bench]] name = "goalzc-crit", harness = false` entry.
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`: new bench.
  - Bench-local `PingPongZC { peer_id }` actor — a copy of the
    `goalzc` bin's actor. Same five-line `handle_message` body
    (`get_msg(msg.len())` then `ctx.send`).
  - `bench_goalzc` builds a fresh `Pool::new(64, 4)` /
    `RuntimeZC` / `ActorManager` per `iter_custom` call,
    seeds `actor 0`, runs 50 ms warmup + 100 ms measurement
    via `run_no_probe`, sums per-actor counts, and scales the
    reported duration to `measurement * iters / total_count`.
  - `Throughput::Elements(1)` — criterion's throughput row
    reads as msgs / s. Single fixed `SIZE = 64`; size-sweeping
    is a future tweak.
  - 10 s `measurement_time`, 50 samples — same dial as
    `goal2-crit`.

### Smoke results (`--size 64`, unpinned)

Apples-to-apples comparison: criterion's defaults give the
bench ~3 s warmup + 10 s measurement, so match it on the bin
side with `goalzc 10 -w 3`.

- `goalzc 10 -w 3 --size 64`: 281 K msg/s (steady state,
  long-lived threads).
- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: median ~232 K msg/s, criterion CI
  [222, 246] K msg/s.
- Ratio goalzc-crit / goalzc ≈ 0.825 — wider than the
  goal2 / goal2-crit reference (~0.95).
- The bot thinks the gap is structural to `iter_custom`: each
  criterion sample is a fresh `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` cycle
  (spawn threads → 50 ms warmup → 100 ms measure → join), so
  the CPU never reaches the warm cache / frequency state that
  `goalzc`'s single set of long-lived threads settles into.
  The 50 ms per-sample warmup just isn't enough to overcome
  cold-spawn state. Not a probe-contamination issue (the
  bench uses `run_no_probe`); it's the cost of criterion's
  "fresh runtime per sample" pattern.
- Future tweak: extend per-sample warmup (e.g. 200 ms) and
  reduce `sample_size` to keep total bench time bounded;
  trade narrower gap for fewer samples.

### Design decisions recorded here

- `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`, not `run`.
  - Direct lesson from `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination —
    the probe-free path exists for exactly this caller.
  - `run` would secretly measure `work + tprobe` instead of
    `work` alone; numbers would be inflated and not
    comparable to `goalzc`'s `adj mean min-p99`.

input.new_string:
Adds `goalzc-crit`, a criterion smoke bench for `goalzc`.
Drives the same two-thread two-actor zerocopy ping-pong
workload via `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` so the measurement
is probe-clean. Mirrors `goal2-crit`'s shape (`iter_custom`,
fresh runtime per sample, scale to iters).

Scope. `RuntimeZC` is structurally `goal2` with a swapped
payload — same threads, same `mpsc`, same warmup / measure /
shutdown flow — so the runtime style is already validated
by `goal2-crit`. `goalzc-crit`'s job is regression insurance
for the pool-backed payload path ("does it still spin up,
send, and tear down?"), not fidelity-perfect agreement with
the `goalzc` binary. The bench-vs-bin gap question gets
answered for free when the lifecycle refactor lands (planned
0.6.0): warmed long-lived threads + per-sample `run` window
collapses the gap structurally.

file.content:
# actor-x1

An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating
Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978). Part of the
[actor-x1 workspace](../..); see the workspace
[README](../../README.md) for overview, usage, and
contribution conventions.

## Binaries

- **`goal1`** — two actors on one thread, ping-ponging an
  empty message through a shared `VecDeque`.
- **`goal2`** — two actors on two threads, ping-ponging
  over `std::sync::mpsc` channels.

Install from the workspace root:

```
cargo install --path crates/actor-x1
```

## Benches

Criterion cross-validation of the goal1 / goal2 workloads.
Run from this crate directory:

```
cargo bench --bench goal1-crit
cargo bench --bench goal2-crit
```

`goal1-crit` sweeps `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}` to mirror
goal1's `--inner` knob; `goal2-crit` runs a single
two-thread mpsc ping-pong shape. Compare criterion's
per-message time and throughput against goal1 / goal2's
tprobe `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99` / `M msg/s` at
the same config.

## Notes

- [`notes/design.md`](notes/design.md) — staged actor-model
  design. Stage 1 shipped at `0.1.0`; Stage 2 is next.

## License

Dual-licensed under [MIT](LICENSE-MIT) OR
[Apache-2.0](LICENSE-APACHE).

- **`goal1`** — two actors on one thread, ping-ponging an

- **`goal2`** — two actors on two threads, ping-ponging
  over `std::sync::mpsc` channels.
- **`goalzc`** — two zerocopy actors on two threads,
  ping-ponging pool-backed `PooledMsg` buffers (handler view:

  every `get_msg(size)` call inside the handler clears the
  bound by construction.

Criterion cross-validation of the goal1 / goal2 workloads.
Run from this crate directory:

input.new_string:
Criterion cross-validation / smoke benches for the goal1 /
goal2 / goalzc workloads. Run from this crate directory:

```
cargo bench --bench goal1-crit
cargo bench --bench goal2-crit
cargo bench --bench goalzc-crit
```

- `goal1-crit` sweeps `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}` to mirror
  goal1's `--inner` knob.
- `goal2-crit` runs a single two-thread mpsc ping-pong shape.
- `goalzc-crit` runs the zerocopy ping-pong shape via
  `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` so the measurement is
  probe-clean. Smoke-bench scope: regression insurance for
  the pool-backed payload path, not fidelity-perfect
  agreement with the `goalzc` binary (the bench's
  fresh-runtime-per-sample shape never reaches `goalzc`'s
  long-lived steady state — collapses out when the lifecycle
  refactor lands in 0.6.0).

Compare criterion's per-message time and throughput against
the corresponding bin's tprobe `mean min-p99` / `adj mean
min-p99` / `M msg/s` at the same config.

# actor-x1

An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating
Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978). Part of the
[actor-x1 workspace](../..); see the workspace
[README](../../README.md) for overview, usage, and
contribution conventions.

## Binaries

- **`goal1`** — two actors on one thread, ping-ponging an
  empty message through a shared `VecDeque`.
- **`goal2`** — two actors on two threads, ping-ponging
  over `std::sync::mpsc` channels.

Install from the workspace root:

```
cargo install --path crates/actor-x1
```

## Benches

Criterion cross-validation of the goal1 / goal2 workloads.
Run from this crate directory:

```
cargo bench --bench goal1-crit
cargo bench --bench goal2-crit
```

`goal1-crit` sweeps `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}` to mirror
goal1's `--inner` knob; `goal2-crit` runs a single
two-thread mpsc ping-pong shape. Compare criterion's
per-message time and throughput against goal1 / goal2's
tprobe `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99` / `M msg/s` at
the same config.

## Notes

- [`notes/design.md`](notes/design.md) — staged actor-model
  design. Stage 1 shipped at `0.1.0`; Stage 2 is next.

## License

Dual-licensed under [MIT](LICENSE-MIT) OR
[Apache-2.0](LICENSE-APACHE).

  - 0.5.0-4 `benches/goalzc-crit.rs` — done
  - 0.5.0 close — pending
  - See [[21]] for as-built API surface, per-step starting points, and gotchas (canonical hand-off; supersedes the original `0.5.0-0` plan shape since `-1` and `-2` deviated from it)

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]

## Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0)

Closes the `0.3.0` ladder. The `-N` suffix drops; the probe
now renders raw + `adj mean` side-by-side, the overhead
vocabulary has a formal written definition, each crate
carries its own `notes/`/`README.md`/`LICENSE-*`, `--raw`
and the redundant `CAL_WARMUP` loop are gone, calibration
is unconditional, the `--warmup` default dropped from
10 s → 0.5 s, and the common CLI flags gained short-form
aliases. No behavior change vs `0.3.0-5`; this is the
closing marker plus the README refresh.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.3.0-5` → `0.3.0`.
- `README.md`: sample-output block regenerated from a
  fresh `goal1 0.5 -i 1000 -p 0` run under `0.3.0` — new
  version banner, new apparatus line (now includes
  `loop_per_iter`), new band-table layout with the
  `adj mean` column and `ADJ_SEP`-spaced alignment.
  `### Columns` gains an `adj mean` bullet describing the
  raw-vs-adjusted split; `### Summary rows` notes that
  `adj mean` applies on `mean` / `mean min-p99` and stays
  blank on `stdev` / `stdev min-p99` (shift-invariant).
  `### The apparatus: line` rewritten around the new
  three-component format (`framing`, `loop_per_iter`,
  `per-event at inner=N`), pointing at
  `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`. `Typical
  invocations` updated to use the new short aliases
  (`-w`, `-i`, `-p`). The `The bot's understanding` entry
  for `goal1 --inner 1 shows ~9 ns; --inner 1000 shows
  ~5 ns` rewritten around the raw-vs-`adj mean` story.
  Trailing reference to `--raw` in the "Numbers travel
  with the hardware" section → `--warmup`.
- `notes/todo.md`: `0.3.0-5` entry moves from
  `## In Progress` to `## Done`; a new overall entry
  `Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]` is
  added to `## Done` pointing at this closing section
  (same pattern Stage 1 / 0.2.0 used).
- `notes/chores-01.md`: this section.

### The 0.3.0 ladder at a glance

| Version     | Landmark                                                  |
|---          |---                                                        |
| `0.3.0-0`   | Plan marker + linkme/inventory future-work note           |
| `0.3.0-1`   | Per-crate `notes/` + `README.md` + `LICENSE-*`; `overhead-model.md` written |
| `0.3.0-2`   | `Overhead::per_event_ticks` extended to framing + loop_per_iter; apparatus line shows both |
| `0.3.0-3`   | `TProbe::report` stores raw; band-table gains `adj mean` column |
| `0.3.0-4`   | `--raw` flag removed; `CAL_WARMUP` loop removed; `calibrate()` documents caller-warmup contract |
| `0.3.0-5`   | `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s; `-w/-i/-p` short aliases |
| **`0.3.0`** | **Closing marker**                                        |

### What ships in 0.3.0

- Band-table report with raw hardware-tick columns on the
  left (`first` / `last` / `range` / `count` / `mean`) plus
  a rightmost `adj mean` column showing the
  apparatus-corrected value. `stdev` rows leave the `adj`
  column blank (shift-invariant). 8-space separator between
  `mean` and `adj mean` so the raw-vs-derived distinction
  is visually obvious.

content.content:
410	- `0.5.0-1` — `Pool` + `PooledMsg` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo`. Done & committed.
411	- `0.5.0-2` — `actor_manager.rs` (catalog + traits) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport). Done; awaiting commit at hand-off time.
412	- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`. Pending.
413	- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`. Pending.
414	- `0.5.0` — closing marker. Pending.
415	
416	### As-built API surface (deviations from `0.5.0-0` baked in)
417	
418	- `Pool::new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32) -> Pool<MutexLifo>` — pre-allocates `msg_count` `Box<[u8]>` of `msg_size` bytes; no lazy growth, no `MAX_SIZE` const.
419	- `pool.get_msg(size: usize) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>` where `PoolError ∈ { SizeTooLarge { requested, max }, NoMsgs }`.
420	- `pool.msg_size() -> u32`, `pool.size() -> usize` — use `msg_size()` for `--size` validation; `NoMsgs` is the saturation signal at runtime.
421	- `crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs`:
422	  - `trait ActorZC<S = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
423	  - `trait ContextZC<S = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`.
424	  - `ActorManager::new(prefix: &str)` / `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` / `take_actors() -> Vec<…>` / `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
425	- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
426	  - `RuntimeZC::new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self` / `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
427	  - `run(&mut self, mgr, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores: &[usize]) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.
428	  - `run_no_probe(...) -> Vec<u64>` — same orchestration, probe-free hot loop.
429	- App pattern (drives both `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit`):
430	  ```rust
431	  let pool = Pool::new(size, 4);                    // 4 buffers ≫ ping-pong steady state of 2
432	  let mut rt  = RuntimeZC::new(pool.clone());
433	  let mut mgr = ActorManager::new("goalzc.dispatch");
434	  let a = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 });
435	  let _ = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 0 });
436	  let initial = vec![(a, pool.get_msg(size).expect("seed"))];
437	  let results = rt.run(&mut mgr, initial, warmup, measurement, &pins);
438	  ```
439	
440	### 0.5.0-3 — `bin/goalzc.rs`
441	
442	- CLI mirrors `goal2`'s positional / flags: `duration_s` (positional), `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`.
443	- New flag: `--size N` (`u32`, default 64). Validate `1 ≤ N ≤ pool.msg_size()` at startup; error cleanly if violated. The pool is constructed at runtime, so this is a runtime check, not a const.
444	- Pool sizing: 4 buffers covers ping-pong (steady state is 2). A `--pool-count` flag is a future tweak, not needed for 0.5.0.
445	- Use `RuntimeZC::run` (probe-instrumented).
446	- Output: throughput summary (use `tprobe::fmt::commafmt` or similar) + per-actor band tables. Crib pattern from `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`.
447	- Doc comments on `#[arg]` fields with bullet lists need `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, ...)]` per the CLAUDE.md clap caveat.
448	- Smoke-test post-install: `goalzc 1 --size 64`, `goalzc 1 --size 64 --pin 0,1`.
449	
450	### 0.5.0-4 — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`
451	
452	- Pattern: `iter_custom`, fresh runtime per sample, scaled by `measurement * iters / total_count`. Lift directly from `crates/actor-x1/benches/goal2-crit.rs`.
453	- **Use `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`, not `run`.** The lesson from `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination is the entire reason `run_no_probe` exists; calling `run` here would re-introduce the same bug.
454	- Add `[[bench]]` entry to `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`:
455	  ```
456	  [[bench]]
457	  name = "goalzc-crit"
458	  harness = false
459	  ```

file.content:
- `0.5.0-1` — `Pool` + `PooledMsg` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo`. Done & committed.
- `0.5.0-2` — `actor_manager.rs` (catalog + traits) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport). Done; awaiting commit at hand-off time.
- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0` — closing marker. Pending.

### As-built API surface (deviations from `0.5.0-0` baked in)

- `Pool::new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32) -> Pool<MutexLifo>` — pre-allocates `msg_count` `Box<[u8]>` of `msg_size` bytes; no lazy growth, no `MAX_SIZE` const.
- `pool.get_msg(size: usize) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>` where `PoolError ∈ { SizeTooLarge { requested, max }, NoMsgs }`.
- `pool.msg_size() -> u32`, `pool.size() -> usize` — use `msg_size()` for `--size` validation; `NoMsgs` is the saturation signal at runtime.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs`:
  - `trait ActorZC<S = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `trait ContextZC<S = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`.
  - `ActorManager::new(prefix: &str)` / `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` / `take_actors() -> Vec<…>` / `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - `RuntimeZC::new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self` / `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
  - `run(&mut self, mgr, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores: &[usize]) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.
  - `run_no_probe(...) -> Vec<u64>` — same orchestration, probe-free hot loop.
- App pattern (drives both `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit`):
  ```rust
  let pool = Pool::new(size, 4);                    // 4 buffers ≫ ping-pong steady state of 2
  let mut rt  = RuntimeZC::new(pool.clone());
  let mut mgr = ActorManager::new("goalzc.dispatch");
  let a = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 });
  let _ = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 0 });
  let initial = vec![(a, pool.get_msg(size).expect("seed"))];
  let results = rt.run(&mut mgr, initial, warmup, measurement, &pins);
  ```

### 0.5.0-3 — `bin/goalzc.rs`

- CLI mirrors `goal2`'s positional / flags: `duration_s` (positional), `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`.
- New flag: `--size N` (`u32`, default 64). Validate `1 ≤ N ≤ pool.msg_size()` at startup; error cleanly if violated. The pool is constructed at runtime, so this is a runtime check, not a const.
- Pool sizing: 4 buffers covers ping-pong (steady state is 2). A `--pool-count` flag is a future tweak, not needed for 0.5.0.
- Use `RuntimeZC::run` (probe-instrumented).
- Output: throughput summary (use `tprobe::fmt::commafmt` or similar) + per-actor band tables. Crib pattern from `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`.
- Doc comments on `#[arg]` fields with bullet lists need `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, ...)]` per the CLAUDE.md clap caveat.
- Smoke-test post-install: `goalzc 1 --size 64`, `goalzc 1 --size 64 --pin 0,1`.

### 0.5.0-4 — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`

- Pattern: `iter_custom`, fresh runtime per sample, scaled by `measurement * iters / total_count`. Lift directly from `crates/actor-x1/benches/goal2-crit.rs`.
- **Use `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`, not `run`.** The lesson from `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination is the entire reason `run_no_probe` exists; calling `run` here would re-introduce the same bug.
- Add `[[bench]]` entry to `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`:
  ```
  [[bench]]
  name = "goalzc-crit"
  harness = false
  ```

content.content:
```
  [[bench]]
  name = "goalzc-crit"
  harness = false
  ```
- Default to `--size 64`. Sweeping sizes is optional — `Throughput::Bytes(size as u64)` if size-sweeping; `Throughput::Elements(1)` for fixed-size.
- Cross-check: run `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` at the same size; throughput should agree within the goal2-vs-goal2-crit window (~5–10%; `goal2`/`goal2-crit` reported 228 K vs 240 K msg/s during 0.4.0 development).

### 0.5.0 — close

- Bump `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml` from `0.5.0-N` → `0.5.0`.
- Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md`:
  - Binaries section: add `goalzc` (with `--size` description).
  - Benches section: add `goalzc-crit`.
- `notes/todo.md`: move the In-Progress `[20]` entry to `## Done` with reference `[20]`.
- Final `vc-x1 push main` to land the closing commit.

### Gotchas / non-obvious things

- **Drain invariant**: `pool.free_len() == pool.size()` must hold after every `run` / `run_no_probe` returns. Tests `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` and `run_no_probe_returns_counts` codify this; don't break them.
- **Probe contamination**: never call `RuntimeZC::run` from a criterion bench. Use `run_no_probe`.
- **`--size` validation** hits `pool.msg_size()`, not a const. The bound is set when the pool is built; the CLI pool gets built from the `--size` flag, so the validation is "is `--size` ≤ `pool.msg_size()` after pool construction" — easiest is `Pool::new(size, 4)` then nothing further to validate, since by construction `pool.msg_size() == size`. The validation matters more when sizes are runtime-variable (future multi-size sub-pools).
- **Bullet doc comments on `#[arg]` fields → reflowed to prose by clap unless `verbatim_doc_comment` is set.** See `vc-x1/src/init.rs` for worked examples; the CLAUDE.md "Writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section calls this out.
- **Initial messages are the app's responsibility** — neither manager nor runtime stores them. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` build the seed `PooledMsg` from the same `pool` they pass to `RuntimeZC::new`.
- **`PingPong` test struct**: lives in `runtime_zc::tests`. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` will need their own copy (it's small) or a shared `pub` version under `crate::actors` — leaving as a duplication choice for the next session.

Closes the `0.5.0` ladder. The `-N` suffix drops; the workspace
now ships a pool-backed zerocopy actor runtime (`RuntimeZC`),
an actor catalog (`ActorManager`), the `goalzc` binary, and
the `goalzc-crit` smoke bench. No behavior change vs `0.5.0-4`;
this is the closing marker plus the README refresh.

- `crates/actor-x1/README.md`:
  - **Binaries** section: add `goalzc` bullet with one-line
    description (zerocopy actors, `--size N` payload sweep,
    pool built so `get_msg(size)` clears the bound by
    construction).
  - **Benches** section: add `goalzc-crit`; note the smoke-
    bench scope (regression insurance for the pool-backed
    payload path, not fidelity-perfect agreement with the
    `goalzc` bin) and the fact that the `iter_custom` /
    fresh-runtime-per-sample shape will collapse out when
    the lifecycle refactor lands in `0.6.0`.

  collapses into a single `## Done` line with refs `[[20]],[[21]],[[22]]`
  (plan marker, hand-off, multi-step ladder). `In Progress`
  becomes `(none)` since `0.6.0` has no plan marker yet.

### The 0.5.0 ladder at a glance

| Version     | Landmark                                                                                  |
|---          |---                                                                                        |
| `0.5.0-0`   | Plan marker + multi-step ladder shape                                                     |
| `0.5.0-1`   | `Pool` + `PooledMsg` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo`                                        |
| `0.5.0-2`   | `actor_manager.rs` (catalog + traits) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport, `run` / `run_no_probe`)|
| `0.5.0-3`   | `bin/goalzc.rs` — two-thread zerocopy ping-pong with `--size N`                           |
| `0.5.0-4`   | `benches/goalzc-crit.rs` — criterion smoke bench using `run_no_probe`                     |
| **`0.5.0`** | **Closing marker**                                                                        |

### What ships in 0.5.0

- `Pool<S: BufRefStore>` — fixed-capacity pool of
  `Box<[u8]>` buffers. Default `S = MutexLifo`. `get_msg`
  returns `Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`; `Drop` returns
  the buffer to the pool. No lazy growth; saturation surfaces
  as `PoolError::NoMsgs`.
- `ActorManager<S>` — catalog of zerocopy actors. `add()`
  takes ownership of an `impl ActorZC<S>`, returns its `u32`
  id; `take_actors()` drains for the runtime; metadata
  (`probe_name_prefix`) is held aside.
- `RuntimeZC<S>` — multi-threaded transport. Spawns one
  thread per actor, wires per-actor `mpsc` channels, runs
  the warmup / measure / shutdown lifecycle, joins, returns
  per-actor results. Two entry points: `run` (`tprobe`-
  instrumented, `Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`) and `run_no_probe`
  (probe-clean, `Vec<u64>`).
- `goalzc` binary — two zerocopy actors, ping-pong
  pool-backed buffers over `mpsc`, `--size N` (default 64)
  payload sweep, `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` /
  `--pin` mirroring `goal2`.
- `goalzc-crit` bench — criterion smoke harness for the
  pool-backed payload path. Uses `run_no_probe`; fixed
  `SIZE = 64`, `Throughput::Elements(1)`.

### What '"'"'s next

- `0.6.0-0` — lifecycle refactor on `RuntimeZC`. Split
  `run` into `startup` / `run` / `teardown` so callers can
  amortize spawn / warmup across multiple `run` windows.
  Discussed during the `0.5.0` close session; design notes
  to be captured in the `0.6.0-0` plan-marker section.
- The bot thinks the lifecycle split is the natural
  unblock for the `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` ~17.5% gap
  observed during `0.5.0-4`: a warmed-once long-lived
  runtime measured per-`run` window (rather than per-
  fresh-spawn) closes the gap as a side effect of the
  cleaner API.

file.content:
# actor-x1

An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating
Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978). Part of the
[actor-x1 workspace](../..); see the workspace
[README](../../README.md) for overview, usage, and
contribution conventions.

## Binaries

- **`goal1`** — two actors on one thread, ping-ponging an
  empty message through a shared `VecDeque`.
- **`goal2`** — two actors on two threads, ping-ponging
  over `std::sync::mpsc` channels.
- **`goalzc`** — two zerocopy actors on two threads,
  ping-ponging pool-backed `PooledMsg` buffers (handler view:
  `&[u8]`) over `std::sync::mpsc`. Adds `--size N` (default
  64) to sweep payload size; the pool is built at startup so
  every `get_msg(size)` call inside the handler clears the
  bound by construction.

Install from the workspace root:

```
cargo install --path crates/actor-x1
```

## Benches

Criterion cross-validation / smoke benches for the goal1 /
goal2 / goalzc workloads. Run from this crate directory:

```
cargo bench --bench goal1-crit
cargo bench --bench goal2-crit
cargo bench --bench goalzc-crit
```

- `goal1-crit` sweeps `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}` to mirror
  goal1's `--inner` knob.
- `goal2-crit` runs a single two-thread mpsc ping-pong shape.
- `goalzc-crit` runs the zerocopy ping-pong shape via
  `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` so the measurement is
  probe-clean. Smoke-bench scope: regression insurance for
  the pool-backed payload path, not fidelity-perfect
  agreement with the `goalzc` binary (the bench's
  fresh-runtime-per-sample shape never reaches `goalzc`'s
  long-lived steady state — collapses out when the lifecycle
  refactor lands in 0.6.0).

Compare criterion's per-message time and throughput against
the corresponding bin's tprobe `mean min-p99` / `adj mean
min-p99` / `M msg/s` at the same config.

## Notes

- [`notes/design.md`](notes/design.md) — staged actor-model
  design. Stage 1 shipped at `0.1.0`; Stage 2 is next.

## License

Dual-licensed under [MIT](LICENSE-MIT) OR
[Apache-2.0](LICENSE-APACHE).

3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.

7	
8	(none)
9	
10	## Todo
11	
12	See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)
13	
14	- Initial design [[1]]
15	- PoC implementation of a manager
16	- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
17	  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
18	  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
19	- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
20	  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
21	  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
22	  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
23	  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
24	  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
25	  boost frequency first
26	- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
27	  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
28	  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
29	  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
30	  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
31	  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
32	  expected-scale heuristics
33	- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
34	  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
35	  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
36	  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
37	  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
38	- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
39	  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
40	  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
41	  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
42	  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
43	  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
44	  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
45	  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
46	  once we instrument drain cost):
47	  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
48	    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
49	    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
50	    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
51	    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
52	  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
53	    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
54	    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
55	    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
56	  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
57	    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
58	    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
59	    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
60	    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
61	  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
62	    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
63	    unbounded-growth shape.
64	- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
65	  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
66	      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
67	      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
68	      without the split;
69	  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
70	      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
71	      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
72	      same generated code under `--release`;
73	  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
74	      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

91	- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
92	- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
93	- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

(none)

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]
- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[20]],[[21]],[[22]]

    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.text:
# Plan: rename `run_no_probe` → `run`; `run` → `run_probed` (0.5.1)

Single-step. Mechanical rename + version bump. No behavior change.

## Scope

- `run_no_probe` (probe-clean) becomes `run` — the default
  measurement primitive.
- `run` (probe-instrumented) becomes `run_probed` — explicit
  opt-in for diagnostic instrumentation.
- Frees the `run` name for the narrower meaning it'll take in
  the planned `0.6.0` lifecycle split (just the "process for
  a window" phase).

## Edits

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0` → `0.5.1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - `pub fn run` → `pub fn run_probed`.
  - `pub fn run_no_probe` → `pub fn run`.
  - Internal helpers: `actor_loop_no_probe` → `actor_loop`;
    `actor_loop_probe` → `actor_loop_probed` (keep symmetry
    with public surface).
  - Module-level docstring: rewrite the "two run entry points"
    paragraph in terms of the new names.
  - Tests: `run_no_probe_returns_counts` →
    `run_returns_counts`; `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` and
    `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` switch their `rt.run(...)`
    calls to `rt.run_probed(...)` (they exercise the probe
    path via the `(count, TProbe)` return tuple).
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`: `rt.run(...)` →
  `rt.run_probed(...)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  `rt.run_no_probe(...)` → `rt.run(...)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/README.md`: the Benches section text
  mentioning `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` updates to `run`.
- `notes/todo.md`: add to Done; new ref `[24]`.
- `notes/chores-02.md`: new
  `## Rename run_no_probe → run, run → run_probed (0.5.1)`
  section (file-by-file + rationale).

## Pre-commit checklist

Standard: fmt / clippy / test / install + retest (binaries
report `0.5.1`; smoke-test `goalzc`).

OK to proceed?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# actor-x1

An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating
Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978). Part of the
[actor-x1 workspace](../..); see the workspace
[README](../../README.md) for overview, usage, and
contribution conventions.

## Binaries

- **`goal1`** — two actors on one thread, ping-ponging an
  empty message through a shared `VecDeque`.
- **`goal2`** — two actors on two threads, ping-ponging
  over `std::sync::mpsc` channels.
- **`goalzc`** — two zerocopy actors on two threads,
  ping-ponging pool-backed `PooledMsg` buffers (handler view:
  `&[u8]`) over `std::sync::mpsc`. Adds `--size N` (default
  64) to sweep payload size; the pool is built at startup so
  every `get_msg(size)` call inside the handler clears the
  bound by construction.

Install from the workspace root:

```
cargo install --path crates/actor-x1
```

## Benches

Criterion cross-validation / smoke benches for the goal1 /
goal2 / goalzc workloads. Run from this crate directory:

```
cargo bench --bench goal1-crit
cargo bench --bench goal2-crit
cargo bench --bench goalzc-crit
```

- `goal1-crit` sweeps `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}` to mirror
  goal1's `--inner` knob.
- `goal2-crit` runs a single two-thread mpsc ping-pong shape.
- `goalzc-crit` runs the zerocopy ping-pong shape via
  `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe` so the measurement is
  probe-clean. Smoke-bench scope: regression insurance for
  the pool-backed payload path, not fidelity-perfect
  agreement with the `goalzc` binary (the bench's
  fresh-runtime-per-sample shape never reaches `goalzc`'s
  long-lived steady state — collapses out when the lifecycle
  refactor lands in 0.6.0).

Compare criterion's per-message time and throughput against
the corresponding bin's tprobe `mean min-p99` / `adj mean
min-p99` / `M msg/s` at the same config.

## Notes

- [`notes/design.md`](notes/design.md) — staged actor-model
  design. Stage 1 shipped at `0.1.0`; Stage 2 is next.

## License

Dual-licensed under [MIT](LICENSE-MIT) OR
[Apache-2.0](LICENSE-APACHE).

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

(none)

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]
- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[23]],[[20]],[[21]],[[22]]

content.text:
Proposed `notes/chores-02.md` section:

---

## Rename run_no_probe → run, run → run_probed (0.5.1)

Mechanical rename + version bump. No behavior change.
Probe-clean becomes the default measurement primitive;
probe instrumentation becomes the explicit opt-in. Frees
the `run` name for the narrower "process for a window"
meaning it'll take in the planned `0.6.0` lifecycle split.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0` → `0.5.1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - Public: `run_no_probe` → `run`; `run` → `run_probed`.
  - Internal: `actor_loop_no_probe` → `actor_loop`;
    `actor_loop_probe` → `actor_loop_probed`.
  - Module-level docstring rewritten around the new names.
  - Tests: `run_no_probe_returns_counts` →
    `run_returns_counts`; `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down`
    and `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` switch to
    `rt.run_probed(...)` (they exercise the probe path).
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`: `rt.run(...)` →
  `rt.run_probed(...)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  `rt.run_no_probe(...)` → `rt.run(...)`; module docstring
  updated.
- `crates/actor-x1/README.md`: Benches description for
  `goalzc-crit` updated to mention `RuntimeZC::run`
  (probe-free default).
- `notes/todo.md`: Done entry + `[24]` ref.
- `notes/chores-02.md`: this section.

### Rationale

- Probe-clean is the default measurement primitive — what
  benches and any future production-shaped consumer want.
  Naming it `run` matches that.
- Probe instrumentation is opt-in for diagnostic work — the
  `goalzc` band-table report path. Naming it `run_probed`
  marks the trade explicitly.
- Reads better at call sites: `rt.run(...)` for normal use,
  `rt.run_probed(...)` when you want the histogram. The
  previous pairing inverted the more common case.
- Earlier chores entries (`0.5.0-2` "As-built API surface",
  `0.5.0-4` "Use run_no_probe, not run") still describe the
  pre-rename API correctly for their version; they're not
  rewritten.

---

OK to append?

content.text:
# Plan: 0.6.0 — RuntimeZC lifecycle (startup / run / teardown)

Multi-step ladder. Splits the all-in-one `run` / `run_probed`
into a caller-orchestrated lifecycle so spawn / warmup
amortize across multiple `run` windows.

## Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // call as many times as wanted
let counts = handle.teardown();
```

Probe variant: `rt.startup_probed(...) -> ProbedHandle`
whose `teardown() -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.

## Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (chores section + `0.6.0-0`
  version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::teardown`; tests updated; old
  all-in-one `run` kept as a thin wrapper for the simple
  case.
- `0.6.0-2` — `ProbedHandle` + `startup_probed`; `goalzc`
  bin moves over (or stays on the wrapper).
- `0.6.0-3` — `goalzc-crit` calls `startup` once,
  `run(window)` per criterion sample, `teardown` once.
  Payoff: bench measures steady-state work only — the
  ~17.5% gap collapses.
- `0.6.0` — close.

## Defaults to review before coding

- **Counter sampling**: per-actor `Arc<AtomicU64>` (relaxed
  `fetch_add` on each dispatch). Cheap at our cross-thread
  scale. Alternative is signal-and-reply per `run`; rejected
  as more API for less benefit.
- **Handle ownership**: `Handle::Drop` sends Shutdown if
  `teardown` was forgotten. Caller still gets the safe
  pattern by calling `teardown()` for results.
- **Probe selection**: separate `startup` /
  `startup_probed` returning distinct `Handle` /
  `ProbedHandle` types. Keeps return-type ergonomics simple
  vs one method with a flag.
- **`ClearProbe` boundary**: caller-driven via
  `probed_handle.clear_probes()`. Removes the implicit
  "first run window is warmup" magic.
- **All-in-one `run` / `run_probed`**: survive as thin
  composition wrappers (startup + optional sleep + clear +
  run + teardown). Minimizes churn in tests / callers that
  don't want fine control.
- **Trait or methods**: methods on `RuntimeZC`. Trait later,
  when a second runtime impl appears.

## Open questions

- Should `Handle` carry the pool handle for ad-hoc post-
  startup message injection? Probably yes.
- `clear_probes()` per-actor or all-at-once? All-at-once
  matches today's behavior.

OK to lock these in and start with `0.6.0-0`?

message.content: This will be using "run" so no probing in rutime but it does need to support a "stop" method right now but soon "stop" will be handled by a typed message to the runtime it self which is how the ActorManager will stop it gracefully and for none graceful stops we signal the thread, thoughts?

content.text:
Makes sense. The three implications:

- **Probe out of runtime** ⇒ `goalzc`'s diagnostic role moves to a `ProbedActor<A>` wrapper that brackets `handle_message` with a `tprobe` scope. The runtime sees only `dyn ActorZC`; the wrapper exposes a `take_probe()` after `stop`. New small piece of code.
- **`stop` as the primitive** ⇒ today's `Shutdown` signal *is* `stop`'s implementation; post-0.6.0 the typed message *replaces* the signal but the public `Handle::stop` API stays. Naming aligned now → less churn later.
- **Non-graceful via thread signal** ⇒ deferred to post-0.6.0. No work this ladder.

Open questions before I rewrite the plan:

- **`Handle::run(window)` return shape**: per-window count deltas (snapshot before/after `Arc<AtomicU64>`) or cumulative-only at `stop`? Per-window deltas is cleaner for benches and what `goalzc-crit` wants. **Vote: per-window deltas.**
- **`ProbedActor` timing**: land in `0.6.0` (so the runtime is fully probe-free at close, `goalzc` migrates) or defer to `0.7.0` (keep `run_probed` as a stub one more version)? **Vote: land in `0.6.0`.**
- **All-in-one wrappers `run(warmup, measurement, ...)`**: keep as ergonomics for tests / simple bins, or rip out at close? Tests + `goalzc` would each grow a few lines without them. **Vote: keep one all-in-one wrapper, drop the probe-aware variant.**

If those votes sound right, I'll rewrite the plan and propose the `0.6.0-0` plan-marker commit.

content: We're starting the 0.6.0 ladder: a RuntimeZC lifecycle refactor (startup/run/stop) with probing moved out of the runtime. Next action: you confirm three open votes (per-window count deltas, ProbedActor lands in 0.6.0, keep one all-in-one wrapper) so I can write the 0.6.0-0 plan marker.

message.content: I'm not conserned with run_probed or probing "all actors" generally, it can be done, as you said with a wrapping trait but we don't need to worry about it. On the count I think simple cheap a non-atomic counter kept per RuntimeZC instance, set to zero on startup and the value is returned by the "stop" API, thoughts?

content.text:
Sounds right. Reading the shape:

- **Counter mechanics**: each actor thread keeps a local `u64`, increments inline (no atomic, no shared write). On `Shutdown` recv, the thread returns its count via its `JoinHandle`. The runtime sums on `stop` and returns one aggregate `u64`. No atomic ops in the hot path; `join` provides the only synchronization. Effectively today's `Vec<u64>` collapsed to a `u64` sum at the boundary.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window. Counts aren't readable mid-flight (would require atomics or signal-and-reply, both contrary to "simple cheap").
- **Aggregate vs per-actor**: with `stop -> u64` aggregate, the per-actor breakdown today's `goalzc` prints goes away. Since you said probing isn't a concern, that's fine.
- **`goalzc-crit`**: keeps its current shape (one `startup` / `run` / `stop` cycle per criterion sample) using the new API. Amortization-via-long-lived-runtime stays unrealized this version because counts can't be sampled mid-flight without atomics. Acceptable — the runtime API is the priority.

One thing worth pinning before I rewrite the plan:

- **Aggregate `u64` at stop, or `Vec<u64>` per-actor?** Aggregate is the literal reading of "the value". Per-actor preserves a tiny bit of debugging info (which actor handled how many) at zero hot-path cost — the threads already produce per-actor values; we'd just skip the sum. Vote either way. **My vote: `Vec<u64>` per-actor**, since it's free and a future caller might want it.

If that's settled, I'll rewrite the plan: probe-free runtime, `stop` primitive, `Handle::run(window) -> ()`, counts-at-stop only, `0.6.0-0` plan marker first.

lastPrompt: I'm not conserned with run_probed or probing "all actors" generally, it can be done, as you said with a wrapping trait but we don't need to worry about it. On the count I think simple cheap a non-atom…

message.content: I vote simple one count per thread if people want a per actor then they can use a TBD TProbe::Counter, KIS

content.text:
KIS confirmed. Here's the plan to lock in:

# Plan: 0.6.0 — RuntimeZC lifecycle (startup / run / stop)

Multi-step. Splits the runtime into a caller-orchestrated
lifecycle. Probe support leaves the runtime entirely (TBD
later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait —
not this ladder). `stop` is the explicit graceful primitive,
matching the post-0.6.0 typed-message-to-runtime story.

## Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
```

## Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table (no
  probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
  just through the new API.
- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- `0.6.0` — close.

## Decisions locked in

- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
  concern.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps. Counts
  not readable mid-flight by design.
- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
  panic-during-drop).
- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
  goes with it.
- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**. Trait when a second runtime
  impl appears.

## Open question

- Does `Handle` carry the pool? Caller likely wants
  `handle.pool()` for ad-hoc post-startup `get_msg`.
  **Default: yes.**

OK to lock this in and start with `0.6.0-0` (version bump to `0.6.0-0` + this plan in chores)?
Executing. Creating chores-03.md (new file for the 0.6.0 ladder, following the 0.4→0.5 precedent), bumping to 0.6.0-0, updating todo.

[package]
name = "actor-x1"
version = "0.5.1"
edition = "2024"
description = "An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978)."
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"

[dependencies]
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "wrap_help"] }
tprobe = { path = "../tprobe" }

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = "0.5"

[[bench]]
name = "goal1-crit"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "goal2-crit"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "goalzc-crit"
harness = false

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

(none)

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]
- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[23]],[[20]],[[21]],[[22]]
- Rename `run_no_probe` → `run`, `run` → `run_probed` (0.5.1) [[24]]

content.text:
All clean. fmt + clippy `-D warnings` pass; 28 actor-x1 + 18 tprobe tests pass. Plan-marker is docs-only (no code change beyond the version bump), so install/retest is not informative — skipping.

**Changes**
- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.1` → `0.6.0-0`.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: new file. Plan-marker section
  `## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)` with shape, ladder, locked decisions, open defaults.
- `notes/todo.md`: new In-Progress entry for the 0.6.0 ladder + `[25]` ref to the plan-marker section.
- `Cargo.lock`: regen.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

Proposed commit:

**Title**
```
chore: RuntimeZC lifecycle plan marker (0.6.0-0)
```

**Body**
```
Plan marker for the 0.6.0 multi-step ladder. Splits the
all-in-one run / run_probed into a caller-orchestrated
lifecycle (startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop). Probe
support leaves the runtime; re-enters later via
TProbe::Counter or an actor-wrapper trait. stop is the
explicit graceful primitive matching the post-0.6.0
typed-message-to-runtime story.

- crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml: 0.5.1 → 0.6.0-0.
- notes/chores-03.md: new file; "## RuntimeZC lifecycle:
  startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)" with
  shape, ladder, locked decisions, and open defaults.
- notes/todo.md: new In-Progress entry for the 0.6.0
  ladder + [25] ref.
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main` with this title and body?

content.content:
1	//! Multi-threaded zerocopy runtime — message-moving
2	//! infrastructure.
3	//!
4	//! Pairs with [`crate::actor_manager`], which owns the actor
5	//! model surface ([`ActorZC`], [`ContextZC`], [`ActorManager`]).
6	//! This module is one consumer of that surface; future
7	//! transports (M:N scheduler, alternative channel types, …)
8	//! would be peers.
9	//!
10	//! Responsibilities held here:
11	//!
12	//! - Owns the [`Pool<S>`]; spawns actor threads; wires
13	//!   per-actor `mpsc` channels; runs the warmup / measure /
14	//!   shutdown lifecycle.
15	//! - Knows nothing about "seeds", named lookups, or what a
16	//!   benchmark is. Initial messages are passed to
17	//!   [`RuntimeZC::run`] directly by the app; the runtime
18	//!   delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
19	//! - Drains the manager via [`ActorManager::take_actors`] +
20	//!   [`ActorManager::probe_name_prefix`] when `run` is called.
21	//!
22	//! Two run entry points on [`RuntimeZC`]:
23	//!
24	//! - [`RuntimeZC::run`] — probe-free; returns `count` per
25	//!   actor. The default measurement primitive (used by the
26	//!   `goalzc-crit` criterion bench so measurement is not
27	//!   contaminated by probe overhead).
28	//! - [`RuntimeZC::run_probed`] — `tprobe`-instrumented;
29	//!   returns `(count, TProbe)` per actor. Opt-in for
30	//!   diagnostic instrumentation (used by the `goalzc` binary).
31	//!
32	//! Lifecycle inside `run` / `run_probed`:
33	//!
34	//! - Drain actors from the Manager.
35	//! - Build per-actor channels.
36	//! - Spawn one thread per actor, optionally pinned.
37	//! - Deliver `initial_messages` over the senders.
38	//! - Sleep `warmup`.
39	//! - Send `ClearProbe` to every actor.
40	//! - Sleep `measurement`.
41	//! - Send `Shutdown` to every actor.
42	//! - Drop main-thread senders so the channels close.
43	//! - Join, return per-actor results.
44	//!
45	//! Drain invariant: by the time the join completes, every
46	//! [`PooledMsg`] that was ever in flight has been dropped, so
47	//! `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
48	
49	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender};
50	use std::thread::{self, JoinHandle};
51	use std::time::Duration;
52	
53	use crate::actor_manager::{ActorManager, ActorZC, ContextZC};
54	use crate::pool::{BufRefStore, MutexLifo, Pool, PoolError, PooledMsg};
55	use tprobe::TProbe;
56	
57	/// Internal channel payload.
58	///
59	/// - `User(msg)` — caller-visible traffic.
60	/// - `ClearProbe` — runtime injects at warmup → measurement
61	///   boundary so the report only sees steady-state data.
62	/// - `Shutdown` — runtime injects after the measurement
63	///   window; actor exits its `recv` loop on receipt.
64	enum SignalZC<S: BufRefStore> {
65	    User(PooledMsg<S>),
66	    ClearProbe,
67	    Shutdown,
68	}
69	
70	/// Multi-threaded zerocopy runtime — message-moving
71	/// infrastructure.
72	///
73	/// - Owns the [`Pool<S>`] (cloned cheaply to each actor
74	///   thread).
75	/// - Spawns threads, wires channels, runs the warmup /
76	///   measure / shutdown lifecycle.
77	/// - Knows nothing about "seeds", named lookups, or what's
78	///   being measured. Initial messages come in as a parameter
79	///   to `run` and are delivered after spawn / before warmup.
80	/// - Generic over the store backend `S` so concurrency
81	///   strategies can be swapped and benchmarked. Default
82	///   [`MutexLifo`].
83	pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo> {
84	    pool: Pool<S>,
85	}
86	
87	impl<S: BufRefStore + 'static> RuntimeZC<S> {
88	    /// Build a runtime that draws buffers from `pool`.
89	    ///
90	    /// - The runtime stores a cheap `Arc` clone; the caller
91	    ///   keeps the original handle so it can build initial
92	    ///   messages or inspect (`free_len`, `size`).
93	    pub fn new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self {
94	        Self { pool }
95	    }
96	
97	    /// Borrow the runtime's pool handle.
98	    pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S> {
99	        &self.pool
100	    }
