1	//! Multi-threaded zerocopy runtime — message-moving
2	//! infrastructure.
3	//!
4	//! Pairs with [`crate::actor_manager`], which owns the actor
5	//! model surface ([`ActorZC`], [`ContextZC`], [`ActorManager`]).
6	//! This module is one consumer of that surface; future
7	//! transports (M:N scheduler, alternative channel types, …)
8	//! would be peers.
9	//!
10	//! Two API styles in this version:
11	//!
12	//! - **Lifecycle (preferred, new in `0.6.0-1`)**: caller
13	//!   composes [`RuntimeZC::startup`] →
14	//!   [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
15	//!   [`Handle::stop`]. `stop` returns the aggregate `u64`
16	//!   message count across all actor threads. Probe-free;
17	//!   diagnostic instrumentation will re-enter post-`0.6.0`
18	//!   via an actor-wrapper trait or `TProbe::Counter`.
19	//! - **All-in-one (legacy, removed in `0.6.0-3`)**:
20	//!   [`RuntimeZC::run`] (probe-free, returns per-actor
21	//!   counts) and [`RuntimeZC::run_probed`] (probe-
22	//!   instrumented, returns per-actor `(count, TProbe)`).
23	//!   These manage their own warmup → measurement → shutdown
24	//!   internally; the warmup boundary issues `ClearProbe` so
25	//!   counts and the histogram only see steady-state data.
26	//!
27	//! Drain invariant: by the time the join completes, every
28	//! [`PooledMsg`] that was ever in flight has been dropped, so
29	//! `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`. Holds for both API
30	//! styles.
31	
32	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender};
33	use std::thread::{self, JoinHandle};
34	use std::time::Duration;
35	
36	use crate::actor_manager::{ActorManager, ActorZC, ContextZC};
37	use crate::pool::{BufRefStore, MutexLifo, Pool, PoolError, PooledMsg};
38	use tprobe::TProbe;
39	
40	/// Internal channel payload.
41	///
42	/// - `User(msg)` — caller-visible traffic.
43	/// - `ClearProbe` — runtime injects at warmup → measurement
44	///   boundary so the report only sees steady-state data.
45	/// - `Shutdown` — runtime injects after the measurement
46	///   window; actor exits its `recv` loop on receipt.
47	enum SignalZC<S: BufRefStore> {
48	    User(PooledMsg<S>),
49	    ClearProbe,
50	    Shutdown,
51	}
52	
53	/// Multi-threaded zerocopy runtime — message-moving
54	/// infrastructure.
55	///
56	/// - Owns the [`Pool<S>`] (cloned cheaply to each actor
57	///   thread).
58	/// - Spawns threads, wires channels, runs the warmup /
59	///   measure / shutdown lifecycle.
60	/// - Knows nothing about "seeds", named lookups, or what's
61	///   being measured. Initial messages come in as a parameter
62	///   to `run` and are delivered after spawn / before warmup.
63	/// - Generic over the store backend `S` so concurrency
64	///   strategies can be swapped and benchmarked. Default
65	///   [`MutexLifo`].
66	pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo> {
67	    pool: Pool<S>,
68	}
69	
70	impl<S: BufRefStore + 'static> RuntimeZC<S> {
71	    /// Build a runtime that draws buffers from `pool`.
72	    ///
73	    /// - The runtime stores a cheap `Arc` clone; the caller
74	    ///   keeps the original handle so it can build initial
75	    ///   messages or inspect (`free_len`, `size`).
76	    pub fn new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self {
77	        Self { pool }
78	    }
79	
80	    /// Borrow the runtime's pool handle.
81	    pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S> {
82	        &self.pool
83	    }
84	
85	    /// Drain the manager's actors and run with no probe
86	    /// instrumentation. The default measurement primitive.
87	    ///
88	    /// - `initial_messages` are delivered after thread spawn,
89	    ///   before warmup.
90	    /// - Returns per-actor `count` in id order.
91	    /// - Used by the `goalzc-crit` criterion bench so the
92	    ///   measurement is probe-clean. The lesson learned from
93	    ///   `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination: keeping a probe-
94	    ///   free path on the runtime from the start avoids the
95	    ///   trap of a bench that secretly measures `work + tprobe`.
96	    pub fn run(
97	        &mut self,
98	        mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>,
99	        initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>,
100	        warmup: Duration,
101	        measurement: Duration,
102	        pin_cores: &[usize],
103	    ) -> Vec<u64> {
104	        self.run_inner(
105	            mgr,
106	            initial_messages,
107	            warmup,
108	            measurement,
109	            pin_cores,
110	            actor_loop::<S>,
111	        )
112	    }
113	
114	    /// Same orchestration as [`RuntimeZC::run`], plus a
115	    /// per-message [`TProbe`] in the hot loop.
116	    ///
117	    /// - Each actor thread owns a `TProbe` recording per-
118	    ///   `handle_message` latency at `inner=1`.
119	    /// - Returns `(count, probe)` per actor in id order.
120	    /// - Used by the `goalzc` binary's diagnostic report.
121	    ///   Avoid in benches: probe overhead contaminates the
122	    ///   measurement.
123	    pub fn run_probed(
124	        &mut self,
125	        mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>,
126	        initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>,
127	        warmup: Duration,
128	        measurement: Duration,
129	        pin_cores: &[usize],
130	    ) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)> {
131	        self.run_inner(
132	            mgr,
133	            initial_messages,
134	            warmup,
135	            measurement,
136	            pin_cores,
137	            actor_loop_probed::<S>,
138	        )
139	    }
140	
141	    /// Spawn actor threads and deliver initial messages.
142	    /// Shared startup logic for [`RuntimeZC::run`] /
143	    /// [`RuntimeZC::run_probed`] (legacy all-in-one) and
144	    /// [`RuntimeZC::startup`] (lifecycle).
145	    ///
146	    /// - Drains the manager's actors via
147	    ///   [`ActorManager::take_actors`].
148	    /// - Builds one `mpsc` channel per actor; spawns one
149	    ///   thread per actor running `actor_loop`, optionally
150	    ///   pinned via `pin_cores[id % pin_cores.len()]`.
151	    /// - Sends `initial_messages` over the per-actor
152	    ///   senders before returning.
153	    /// - Returns the senders (for `ClearProbe` / `Shutdown`
154	    ///   signaling) and join handles (for the eventual join).
155	    fn spawn_and_seed<R: Send + 'static>(
156	        &self,
157	        mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>,
158	        initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>,
159	        pin_cores: &[usize],
160	        actor_loop: ActorLoopFn<S, R>,
161	    ) -> (Vec<Sender<SignalZC<S>>>, Vec<JoinHandle<R>>) {
162	        let actors = mgr.take_actors();
163	        let n = actors.len();
164	        let pool = self.pool.clone();
165	        let probe_name_prefix = mgr.probe_name_prefix().to_string();
166	
167	        let mut senders: Vec<Sender<SignalZC<S>>> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
168	        let mut receivers: Vec<Option<Receiver<SignalZC<S>>>> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
169	        for _ in 0..n {
170	            let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();
171	            senders.push(tx);
172	            receivers.push(Some(rx));
173	        }
174	
175	        let mut handles: Vec<JoinHandle<R>> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
176	        for (id, actor) in actors.into_iter().enumerate() {
177	            #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
178	            // OK: receivers[id] was set to `Some(...)` in the loop above.
179	            let rx = receivers[id].take().unwrap();
180	            let peers: Vec<Sender<SignalZC<S>>> = senders.to_vec();
181	            let probe_name = format!("{}.actor{}", probe_name_prefix, id);
182	            let pin_target = if pin_cores.is_empty() {
183	                None
184	            } else {
185	                Some(pin_cores[id % pin_cores.len()])
186	            };
187	            let actor_pool = pool.clone();
188	            let h = thread::Builder::new()
189	                .name(format!("actor-zc-{id}"))
190	                .spawn(move || {
191	                    tprobe::pin::pin_current(pin_target);
192	                    actor_loop(actor, rx, peers, actor_pool, probe_name)
193	                })
194	                .expect("failed to spawn actor thread");
195	            handles.push(h);
196	        }
197	
198	        for (dst, msg) in initial_messages {

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop (0.6.0) [[25]]
  - 0.6.0-0 plan marker — done
  - 0.6.0-1 `Handle` + lifecycle methods — pending
  - 0.6.0-2 migrate `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` — pending
  - 0.6.0-3 remove old `run` / `run_probed` — pending
  - 0.6.0 close — pending

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]
- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[23]],[[20]],[[21]],[[22]]
- Rename `run_no_probe` → `run`, `run` → `run_probed` (0.5.1) [[24]]

content.text:
Proposed `notes/chores-03.md` section:

---

## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)

Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
they go away in `0.6.0-3`.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts —
    panics on actor panic).
  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
    panic-during-drop.
  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Design notes

- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
  path.

  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.

  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.

## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)

Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
they go away in `0.6.0-3`.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts — panics
    on actor panic).
  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
    panic-during-drop.
  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Design notes

- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
  path.
- **No mid-flight count read**: `Handle::run(window)`
  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Chores-03.md

Chores for the `0.6.0` ladder onward.

## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)

Plan marker for the `0.6.0` multi-step ladder. Splits the
runtime's all-in-one `run` / `run_probed` into a caller-
orchestrated lifecycle (`startup` / `Handle::run` /
`Handle::stop`). Probe support leaves the runtime entirely;
re-enters later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper
trait (out of scope for this ladder). `stop` is the
explicit graceful primitive, matching the post-`0.6.0`
typed-message-to-runtime story.

### Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
```

### Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table
  (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
  just through the new API.
- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- `0.6.0` — close.

### Decisions locked in

- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
  concern.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window.
  Counts not readable mid-flight by design.
- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
  panic-during-drop).
- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
  goes with it. Probe re-introduction is post-`0.6.0`
  work, via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait.
- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**, not a trait. Trait when a
  second runtime impl appears.

### Defaults that aren't yet locked

- `Handle::pool()` accessor so the caller can inject ad-hoc
  messages post-startup. Default yes; revisit during
  `0.6.0-1` if the API gets ugly.

Starting `0.6.0-2`: migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench to the lifecycle API.

For `goalzc`: drops the band-table (no probing), drops `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals`, becomes a simple smoke generator with msg/s.
For `goalzc-crit`: collapses warmup+measurement into a single `Handle::run(window)` call.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop (0.6.0) [[25]]
  - 0.6.0-0 plan marker — done
  - 0.6.0-1 `Handle` + lifecycle methods — done
  - 0.6.0-2 migrate `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` — pending
  - 0.6.0-3 remove old `run` / `run_probed` — pending
  - 0.6.0 close — pending

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]
- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[23]],[[20]],[[21]],[[22]]

content.text:
Proposed `notes/chores-03.md` section:

---

## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)

`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
  - Output: version banner, throughput line
    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
    table.
  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}`
    only (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
    creates two actors (assertion already pins
    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  - Switch `rt.run` → `rt.startup` /
    `handle.run(window)` / `handle.stop`.
  - Collapse the previous `warmup` (50 ms) +
    `measurement` (100 ms) split into a single 150 ms
    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight
    count read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate
    warmup phase would inflate the average; combining
    keeps the per-msg math honest.
  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
    API and the smoke-bench scope.
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Smoke results (0.6.0-2 bench, `--size 64`, unpinned)

- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: ~233 K msg/s (criterion CI
  [232.77, 237.47] K elem/s). Criterion's change
  detector reports "No change in performance detected"
  vs the pre-migration baseline. Confirms the
  lifecycle API doesn't add overhead.
- `goalzc 0.5` (unpinned): ~229 K msg/s.
- `goalzc 0.5 --pin 0,1`: ~284 K msg/s.

### CLI breaking change in 0.6.0-2

`goalzc`'s `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` flags
are gone. Existing scripts using them will fail at clap
parsing. Documented in this section; recipients can
re-derive throughput by simply running for longer (no
explicit warmup needed in a smoke generator).

---

OK to append?

  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.

## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)

`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
  - Output: version banner, throughput line
    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
    table.
  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}` only
    (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
    creates two actors (assertion already pins
    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  - Switch `rt.run` → `rt.startup` /
    `handle.run(window)` / `handle.stop`.
  - Collapse the previous `warmup` (50 ms) +
    `measurement` (100 ms) split into a single 150 ms
    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight count
    read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate warmup
    phase would inflate the average; combining keeps
    the per-msg math honest.
  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
    API and the smoke-bench scope.
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Smoke results (0.6.0-2 bench, `--size 64`, unpinned)

- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: ~233 K msg/s (criterion CI
  [232.77, 237.47] K elem/s). Criterion's change
  detector reports "No change in performance detected"
  vs the pre-migration baseline. Confirms the
  lifecycle API doesn't add overhead.
- `goalzc 0.5` (unpinned): ~229 K msg/s.
- `goalzc 0.5 --pin 0,1`: ~284 K msg/s.

### CLI breaking change in 0.6.0-2

`goalzc`'s `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` flags
are gone. Existing scripts using them will fail at clap
parsing. Documented in this section; recipients can
re-derive throughput by simply running for longer (no
explicit warmup needed in a smoke generator).

# Chores-03.md

Chores for the `0.6.0` ladder onward.

## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)

Plan marker for the `0.6.0` multi-step ladder. Splits the
runtime's all-in-one `run` / `run_probed` into a caller-
orchestrated lifecycle (`startup` / `Handle::run` /
`Handle::stop`). Probe support leaves the runtime entirely;
re-enters later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper
trait (out of scope for this ladder). `stop` is the
explicit graceful primitive, matching the post-`0.6.0`
typed-message-to-runtime story.

### Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
```

### Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table
  (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
  just through the new API.
- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- `0.6.0` — close.

### Decisions locked in

- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
  concern.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window.
  Counts not readable mid-flight by design.
- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
  panic-during-drop).
- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
  goes with it. Probe re-introduction is post-`0.6.0`
  work, via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait.
- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**, not a trait. Trait when a
  second runtime impl appears.

### Defaults that aren't yet locked

- `Handle::pool()` accessor so the caller can inject ad-hoc
  messages post-startup. Default yes; revisit during
  `0.6.0-1` if the API gets ugly.

## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)

Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
they go away in `0.6.0-3`.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts — panics
    on actor panic).
  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
    panic-during-drop.
  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Design notes

- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
  path.
- **No mid-flight count read**: `Handle::run(window)`
  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.

message.content: Why remove -w,--warmup from goalzc and I was expecting that without the probe removing performance would increase?

lastPrompt: Why remove -w,--warmup from goalzc and I was expecting that without the probe removing performance would increase?

content.text:
Two good points.

**On `--warmup`**: I cut it because the lifecycle API has no count-reset boundary today — counts at `stop` cover *all* messages, warmup included. To preserve the original semantics (don't count warmup), the runtime needs a `Handle::reset_count()` that sends the existing `SignalZC::ClearProbe` signal. The actor loops already zero their per-thread count on receipt — that's how the legacy `run` path implements its warmup → measurement boundary. Tiny addition, ~5 lines. I'd add it to `0.6.0-2` and restore `--warmup` on goalzc plus the warmup/measurement split on goalzc-crit. **My vote: yes, restore.**

**On performance**: the probe per-dispatch cost is ~5.8 ns (framing 21 tk ≈ 5.54 ns + scope cost). At goalzc's microsecond scale (~4.3 µs / msg = ~233 K msg/s), the probe is **~0.14%** of the work — the cross-thread `mpsc::recv` + cache-line bounce dominates. Removing it shouldn't move the needle, and the data confirms it (~232 K → ~233 K, within criterion's CI; "No change in performance detected"). The probe is significant at goal1's single-thread scale (~5 ns / dispatch — there it's a real fraction of the cost), but at goal2 / goalzc cross-thread scale it's rounding error.

If the warmup vote is yes, I'll fold the change in before committing `0.6.0-2`.

message.content: When are we going to incorporate using startup, run & teardown so we can measure just the run?

content.text:
It isn't in `0.6.0` as currently scoped — that's the trade we made when you locked in "no atomics, count returned at `stop` only." Per-sample bench amortization needs *mid-flight* count reads, which requires either atomics or signal-and-reply. With neither, the bench has to keep doing `startup → run → stop` per criterion sample (the shape it has today) and pays the spawn/teardown cost on every sample.

Two paths to enable "measure just the run":

- **Signal-and-reply** (preserves KIS-no-atomics): add `Handle::query_count() -> u64` that sends a `QueryCount(oneshot_tx)` signal to each actor; actors reply with their current local count; `query_count` sums replies. Implementation: one new `SignalZC` variant, one new `Handle` method, ~30 lines. Cost per query: ~µs round-trip (driven by mpsc wake latency). Acceptable since queries happen once per criterion sample, not per dispatch.
- **Per-actor `Arc<AtomicU64>`** (~1 ns relaxed `fetch_add` per dispatch): cheap mid-flight read via `load(Relaxed)`. Cleaner API but contradicts your earlier KIS-no-atomics call.

Either could land as `0.6.0-2.5` (added before the cleanup `0.6.0-3`) or as part of a `0.6.1`. The signal-and-reply path is the more conservative answer to your KIS preference; atomics is the more direct answer to your perf instinct.

**My read**: do signal-and-reply now. It threads cleanly into the `--warmup` restoration (reuse the same signal pattern: `reset_count` is signal, `query_count` is signal-and-reply), gives the bench the amortization payoff this ladder, and keeps the hot path lock-free. We can swap to atomics later if profiling ever shows the round-trip is in the way.

Vote? If yes, I'll add both `Handle::reset_count()` and `Handle::query_count()` to `0.6.0-2` (along with restoring `--warmup`), wire goalzc-crit to use `startup` once + `reset_count → run(window) → query_count` per sample, and report what the amortized number looks like.

wink@3900x 26-04-27T02:11:53.538Z:~/data/prgs/rust/actor-x1 ((main))
$ rg teardown

wink@3900x 26-04-27T02:22:51.080Z:~/data/prgs/rust/actor-x1 ((main))

content.text:
Two threads to untangle.

**The `teardown` orphan**: that's a rename artifact. You asked in the `0.6.0-0` planning round to use `stop` as the primitive — "but it does need to support a 'stop' method right now ... matches the future typed-message-to-runtime story." The chores-02 close mention pre-dates that conversation; it's historical and stale, accurate to the moment it was written. The lifecycle as-built uses `stop`. Per the precedent we set in `0.5.1`'s rename ("earlier chores entries still describe the pre-rename API correctly for their version; they're not rewritten"), I'd leave the chores-02 line alone but add a note to the `0.6.0-0` plan-marker section calling out the `teardown → stop` rename so a reader landing on chores-02 can find the trail.

**The trait + amortization isn't in the ladder**: that's the real gap. Let me propose an updated ladder that makes both visible. Adds two sub-steps and a what's-next:

```
- 0.6.0-0 — plan marker (done)
- 0.6.0-1 — Handle + lifecycle methods (done)
- 0.6.0-2 — migrate goalzc + goalzc-crit; restore --warmup
            via new Handle::reset_count (sends ClearProbe;
            actor loops zero their local count). Bench keeps
            cycle-per-sample shape but with the warmup→meas
            split honoured.
- 0.6.0-3 — Handle::query_count via signal-and-reply
            (oneshot per actor). goalzc-crit uses startup
            once / reset+run+query per criterion sample —
            "measure just the run". Reports the amortized
            number; bench-vs-bin gap collapses (or doesn't,
            and we learn something).
- 0.6.0-4 — introduce Runtime trait. RuntimeZC impls it.
            goalzc / goalzc-crit / tests reach through the
            trait, not the concrete type. SignalZC::ClearProbe
            renamed to ClearCount (probe is gone).
- 0.6.0-5 — remove legacy run / run_probed / actor_loop_probed.
            Runtime is probe-free + lifecycle-only.
- 0.6.0   — close.
```

What's next (`0.7.0+`, in chores-04 when the time comes):

- ActorManager-driven graceful shutdown via typed message to a `Runtime` trait method (replaces the explicit `Handle::stop` call).
- Non-graceful shutdown via thread signal.
- Probe re-introduction via actor-wrapper trait or `TProbe::Counter`.

This is 5 sub-steps + close = 6 commits total in the ladder. Each is independently reviewable, none is more than a few hundred lines.

If the shape looks right, my next action is:

1. Update the `0.6.0-0` plan-marker section in `chores-03.md` to enumerate this ladder + add the `teardown → stop` rename note. (Same trick `0.5.0`'s plan marker used: "the 0.5.0-0 'Shape' subsection above is the original plan — `0.5.0-1` and `-2` deviated from it.")
2. Implement the new `0.6.0-2` shape (current edits + `Handle::reset_count` + `--warmup` restored), commit.
3. Continue down the ladder.

Vote on the ladder shape, and on whether to amend the `0.6.0-0` chores section with the rename note + revised ladder?

content.text:
Right — apologies for misreading your "-1" earlier. We're aligned: `0.6.0-2` is the uncommitted working copy, and the `--warmup` removal lives there. Pure file-edit revision, no git/jj surgery.

Here's the proposed update to the `0.6.0-0` plan-marker section in `chores-03.md` (replaces the existing `### Multi-step ladder` bullets, updates one decision, adds a rename-note subsection):

**Multi-step ladder (revised)**
```
- 0.6.0-0 — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- 0.6.0-1 — Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run /
  Handle::stop; tests updated. Existing run / run_probed
  stay alongside (additive step).
- 0.6.0-2 — Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to
  the lifecycle API. New Handle::reset_count (sends
  ClearProbe; actor loops zero their per-thread count
  on receipt). --warmup restored on goalzc and in
  goalzc-crit's warmup → measurement split. goalzc
  loses its band-table (no probing); keeps the msg/s
  line as a smoke generator.
- 0.6.0-3 — Handle::query_count via signal-and-reply
  (oneshot per actor). goalzc-crit goes amortized:
  startup once / reset_count → run(window) →
  query_count per criterion sample. Reports the new
  amortized number; bench-vs-bin gap collapses (or
  doesn't, and we learn something).
- 0.6.0-4 — Introduce Runtime trait. RuntimeZC impls
  it. goalzc / goalzc-crit / tests reach through the
  trait, not the concrete type. SignalZC::ClearProbe
  renamed to ClearCount (probe is gone).
- 0.6.0-5 — Remove legacy run / run_probed /
  actor_loop_probed. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- 0.6.0   — close.
```

**One decision update** (in `### Decisions locked in`): replace "Methods on `RuntimeZC`, not a trait. Trait when a second runtime impl appears." with:

```
- Runtime trait introduced in 0.6.0-4. Consumers reach
  through the trait so the post-0.6.0 ActorManager-driven
  graceful shutdown work has a clean seam to drop into.
```

**New subsection at the end of the plan-marker section**:

```
### Naming: teardown → stop

The 0.5.0 close section's "What's next" pointer used
the name `teardown` for the lifecycle's shutdown
primitive. During the 0.6.0-0 planning round the name
settled on `stop` — matches the post-0.6.0 typed-
message-to-runtime story. The chores-02 mention of
`teardown` is historical (accurate to the moment it
was written, pre-rename) and is left in place per the
same precedent 0.5.1 set with `run_no_probe → run`.
```

That's the chores edit. Once you approve, the order is:

1. Update `chores-03.md` plan marker.
2. Revise uncommitted `0.6.0-2`: add `Handle::reset_count`, restore `--warmup` on goalzc, restore the warmup → measurement split in goalzc-crit using `reset_count` at the boundary.
3. Update `notes/todo.md` to reflect the revised ladder steps.
4. Re-run pre-commit, summarize, get approval, push.

OK to proceed?

message.content: You suggest counts per actor, my suggestion is counts per thread which happens to also be count's per actor in this change ladder.

content.text:
Right — and the distinction matters for future-proofing. Today thread:actor is 1:1 (`RuntimeZC` spawns one thread per actor), so "per-thread" and "per-actor" coincide. But the runtime API should describe what it *actually* knows about: threads. A future M:N runtime would map many actors onto fewer threads, at which point per-thread counters stay correct and "per-actor" loses its meaning until something downstream (an actor-level wrapper like `TProbe::Counter`) breaks them down.

The existing `0.6.0-0` plan-marker section already gets this right where it counts:

> **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS; per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter` concern.

The slip is in my proposed `0.6.0-3` ladder bullet — "oneshot per actor" should be "oneshot per thread". Revised line:

```
- 0.6.0-3 — Handle::query_count via signal-and-reply
  (oneshot per thread). goalzc-crit goes amortized:
  startup once / reset_count → run(window) →
  query_count per criterion sample. Reports the new
  amortized number; bench-vs-bin gap collapses (or
  doesn't, and we learn something).
```

Same for `0.6.0-2`'s `Handle::reset_count` description — say "each thread zeros its local count" not "each actor". Updated bullet:

```
- 0.6.0-2 — Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to
  the lifecycle API. New Handle::reset_count (sends
  ClearProbe; each actor thread zeros its per-thread
  count on receipt). --warmup restored on goalzc and in
  goalzc-crit's warmup → measurement split. goalzc loses
  its band-table (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a
  smoke generator.
```

I'll use "per-thread" consistently in new code/docs/commit text. Existing committed text (chores-02, chores-03 0.6.0-0/0.6.0-1) already uses the right framing in the load-bearing places; nothing to backfill there.

Sound right? On approval I update chores-03 with these corrected bullets + the `teardown → stop` note, then revise the uncommitted `0.6.0-2`.

1	# Chores-03.md
2	
3	Chores for the `0.6.0` ladder onward.
4	
5	## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)
6	
7	Plan marker for the `0.6.0` multi-step ladder. Splits the
8	runtime's all-in-one `run` / `run_probed` into a caller-
9	orchestrated lifecycle (`startup` / `Handle::run` /
10	`Handle::stop`). Probe support leaves the runtime entirely;
11	re-enters later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper
12	trait (out of scope for this ladder). `stop` is the
13	explicit graceful primitive, matching the post-`0.6.0`
14	typed-message-to-runtime story.
15	
16	### Shape
17	
18	```
19	let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
20	handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
21	let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
22	```
23	
24	### Multi-step ladder
25	
26	- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
27	- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
28	  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
29	  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
30	- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
31	  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table
32	  (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
33	  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
34	  just through the new API.
35	- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
36	  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
37	  lifecycle-only.
38	- `0.6.0` — close.
39	
40	### Decisions locked in
41	
42	- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
43	  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
44	  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
45	  concern.
46	- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window.
47	  Counts not readable mid-flight by design.
48	- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
49	  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
50	  panic-during-drop).
51	- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
52	  goes with it. Probe re-introduction is post-`0.6.0`
53	  work, via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait.
54	- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**, not a trait. Trait when a
55	  second runtime impl appears.
56	
57	### Defaults that aren't yet locked
58	
59	- `Handle::pool()` accessor so the caller can inject ad-hoc
60	  messages post-startup. Default yes; revisit during
61	  `0.6.0-1` if the API gets ugly.
62	
63	## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)
64	
65	Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
66	`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
67	[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
68	[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
69	joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
70	`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
71	paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
72	they go away in `0.6.0-3`.
73	
74	- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
75	- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
76	  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
77	    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
78	    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
79	    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
80	    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
81	    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
82	  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
83	    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
84	  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
85	    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
86	    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
87	    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
88	    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts — panics
89	    on actor panic).
90	  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
91	    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
92	    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
93	    panic-during-drop.
94	  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
95	    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
96	  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
97	    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
98	    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
99	    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
100	    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
101	    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
102	- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
103	- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.
104	
105	### Design notes
106	
107	- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
108	  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
109	  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
110	  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
111	  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
112	- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
113	  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
114	  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
115	  path.
116	- **No mid-flight count read**: `Handle::run(window)`
117	  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
118	  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
119	  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
120	- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
121	  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
122	  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
123	  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
124	- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
125	  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
126	  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
127	  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
128	  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
129	  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.
130	
131	### What's not in 0.6.0-1
132	
133	- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
134	  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
135	- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
136	  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
137	  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.
138	
139	## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)
140	
141	`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
142	`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
143	(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
144	loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
145	re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
146	that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
147	cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.
148	
149	- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
150	- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
151	  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
152	    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
153	    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
154	  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
155	    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
156	  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
157	    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
158	  - Output: version banner, throughput line
159	    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
160	    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
161	    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
162	    table.
163	  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}` only
164	    (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
165	    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
166	  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
167	    creates two actors (assertion already pins
168	    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
169	- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
170	  - Switch `rt.run` → `rt.startup` /
171	    `handle.run(window)` / `handle.stop`.
172	  - Collapse the previous `warmup` (50 ms) +
173	    `measurement` (100 ms) split into a single 150 ms
174	    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight count
175	    read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate warmup
176	    phase would inflate the average; combining keeps
177	    the per-msg math honest.
178	  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
179	    API and the smoke-bench scope.
180	- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
181	- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.
182	
183	### Smoke results (0.6.0-2 bench, `--size 64`, unpinned)
184	
185	- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: ~233 K msg/s (criterion CI
186	  [232.77, 237.47] K elem/s). Criterion's change
187	  detector reports "No change in performance detected"
188	  vs the pre-migration baseline. Confirms the
189	  lifecycle API doesn't add overhead.
190	- `goalzc 0.5` (unpinned): ~229 K msg/s.
191	- `goalzc 0.5 --pin 0,1`: ~284 K msg/s.
192	
193	### CLI breaking change in 0.6.0-2
194	
195	`goalzc`'s `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` flags
196	are gone. Existing scripts using them will fail at clap
197	parsing. Documented in this section; recipients can
198	re-derive throughput by simply running for longer (no
199	explicit warmup needed in a smoke generator).

# Chores-03.md

Chores for the `0.6.0` ladder onward.

## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)

Plan marker for the `0.6.0` multi-step ladder. Splits the
runtime's all-in-one `run` / `run_probed` into a caller-
orchestrated lifecycle (`startup` / `Handle::run` /
`Handle::stop`). Probe support leaves the runtime entirely;
re-enters later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper
trait (out of scope for this ladder). `stop` is the
explicit graceful primitive, matching the post-`0.6.0`
typed-message-to-runtime story.

### Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
```

### Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table
  (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
  just through the new API.
- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- `0.6.0` — close.

### Decisions locked in

- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
  concern.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window.
  Counts not readable mid-flight by design.
- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
  panic-during-drop).
- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
  goes with it. Probe re-introduction is post-`0.6.0`
  work, via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait.
- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**, not a trait. Trait when a
  second runtime impl appears.

### Defaults that aren't yet locked

- `Handle::pool()` accessor so the caller can inject ad-hoc
  messages post-startup. Default yes; revisit during
  `0.6.0-1` if the API gets ugly.

## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)

Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
they go away in `0.6.0-3`.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts — panics
    on actor panic).
  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
    panic-during-drop.
  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Design notes

- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
  path.
- **No mid-flight count read**: `Handle::run(window)`
  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.

## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)

`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
  - Output: version banner, throughput line
    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
    table.
  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}` only
    (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
    creates two actors (assertion already pins
    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  - Switch `rt.run` → `rt.startup` /
    `handle.run(window)` / `handle.stop`.
  - Collapse the previous `warmup` (50 ms) +
    `measurement` (100 ms) split into a single 150 ms
    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight count
    read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate warmup
    phase would inflate the average; combining keeps
    the per-msg math honest.
  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
    API and the smoke-bench scope.
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Smoke results (0.6.0-2 bench, `--size 64`, unpinned)

- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: ~233 K msg/s (criterion CI
  [232.77, 237.47] K elem/s). Criterion's change
  detector reports "No change in performance detected"
  vs the pre-migration baseline. Confirms the
  lifecycle API doesn't add overhead.
- `goalzc 0.5` (unpinned): ~229 K msg/s.
- `goalzc 0.5 --pin 0,1`: ~284 K msg/s.

### CLI breaking change in 0.6.0-2

`goalzc`'s `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` flags
are gone. Existing scripts using them will fail at clap
parsing. Documented in this section; recipients can
re-derive throughput by simply running for longer (no
explicit warmup needed in a smoke generator).

# Chores-03.md

Chores for the `0.6.0` ladder onward.

## RuntimeZC lifecycle: startup / run / stop — plan marker (0.6.0-0)

Plan marker for the `0.6.0` multi-step ladder. Splits the
runtime's all-in-one `run` / `run_probed` into a caller-
orchestrated lifecycle (`startup` / `Handle::run` /
`Handle::stop`). Probe support leaves the runtime entirely;
re-enters later via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper
trait (out of scope for this ladder). `stop` is the
explicit graceful primitive, matching the post-`0.6.0`
typed-message-to-runtime story.

### Shape

```
let handle = rt.startup(&mut mgr, initial, pin_cores);
handle.run(window);          // optional, repeatable
let total = handle.stop();   // u64 aggregate; sends Shutdown
```

### Multi-step ladder

- `0.6.0-0` — plan marker (this section + version bump).
- `0.6.0-1` — `Handle` + `RuntimeZC::startup` /
  `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`; tests updated. Existing
  `run` / `run_probed` stay alongside (additive step).
- `0.6.0-2` — Migrate `goalzc` bin and `goalzc-crit` bench
  to the lifecycle API. `goalzc` loses its band-table
  (no probing); keeps the msg/s line as a smoke generator.
  `goalzc-crit` keeps the current cycle-per-sample shape,
  just through the new API.
- `0.6.0-3` — Remove `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` and
  `actor_loop_probed`. Runtime is probe-free +
  lifecycle-only.
- `0.6.0` — close.

### Decisions locked in

- **Counter**: per-thread local `u64`, no atomics. Returned
  via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. `stop -> u64` aggregate. KIS;
  per-actor breakdown is a future `TProbe::Counter`
  concern.
- **`Handle::run(window) -> ()`** — just sleeps the window.
  Counts not readable mid-flight by design.
- **`Handle::Drop`** calls `stop` if forgotten; result is
  discarded. Join errors are swallowed (drop must not
  panic-during-drop).
- **Probing leaves the runtime.** `goalzc`'s band-table
  goes with it. Probe re-introduction is post-`0.6.0`
  work, via `TProbe::Counter` or an actor-wrapper trait.
- **Methods on `RuntimeZC`**, not a trait. Trait when a
  second runtime impl appears.

### Defaults that aren't yet locked

- `Handle::pool()` accessor so the caller can inject ad-hoc
  messages post-startup. Default yes; revisit during
  `0.6.0-1` if the API gets ugly.

## Handle + RuntimeZC::startup / Handle::run / Handle::stop (0.6.0-1)

Adds the lifecycle API alongside the existing all-in-one
`run` / `run_probed`. Caller drives the actor mesh through
[`startup`] → [`Handle::run`] (any number of windows) →
[`Handle::stop`] (returns the aggregate `u64` count and
joins). Probe-free; counts come from per-thread local
`u64`s summed via `JoinHandle` at `stop`. The all-in-one
paths still work (refactored to share spawn / seed logic);
they go away in `0.6.0-3`.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-0` → `0.6.0-1`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - New private `RuntimeZC::spawn_and_seed<R>` extracts
    the spawn + channel-wire + initial-message-deliver
    logic shared by the all-in-one `run_inner` and the
    new `startup`. `run_inner` is now a thin wrapper that
    composes `spawn_and_seed` with the warmup / clear /
    measurement / shutdown / join sequence.
  - New `pub fn RuntimeZC::startup(mgr, initial, pin) ->
    Handle<S>` returns a live handle to the actor mesh.
  - New `pub struct Handle<S = MutexLifo>` with
    `pub fn run(&self, window: Duration)` (just sleeps),
    `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<S>` (caller injection /
    inspection), `pub fn stop(self) -> u64` (sends
    `Shutdown`, joins, sums per-thread counts — panics
    on actor panic).
  - `impl Drop for Handle` performs silent graceful
    shutdown if `stop` was not called: sends `Shutdown`,
    joins, swallows panics and counts. Drop must not
    panic-during-drop.
  - Module-level docstring: rewritten around the two API
    styles (Lifecycle preferred / All-in-one legacy).
  - Tests: `lifecycle_round_trip_returns_total`,
    `lifecycle_multiple_run_windows_accumulate`,
    `lifecycle_handle_pool_accessor`,
    `lifecycle_handle_drop_shuts_down_cleanly`. All four
    validate the drain invariant (`pool.free_len() ==
    pool.size()` after the handle goes away).
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-1` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Design notes

- **`Handle`'s state**: `senders: Option<Vec<Sender<...>>>`
  and `handles: Option<Vec<JoinHandle<u64>>>`. `stop`
  consumes via `take()`; `Drop` checks the same `Option`s
  and runs the silent path only if `stop` didn't.
  Simpler than typestate; KIS.
- **Counter aggregation**: each actor thread keeps a
  local `u64`, returned via `JoinHandle::Output`. `stop`
  sums them with `saturating_add`. No atomics in the hot
  path.
- **No mid-flight count read**: `Handle::run(window)`
  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.

## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)

`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
  - Output: version banner, throughput line
    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
    table.
  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}` only
    (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
    creates two actors (assertion already pins
    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:
  - Switch `rt.run` → `rt.startup` /
    `handle.run(window)` / `handle.stop`.
  - Collapse the previous `warmup` (50 ms) +
    `measurement` (100 ms) split into a single 150 ms
    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight count
    read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate warmup
    phase would inflate the average; combining keeps
    the per-msg math honest.
  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
    API and the smoke-bench scope.
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.

### Smoke results (0.6.0-2 bench, `--size 64`, unpinned)

- `goalzc-crit/pingpong`: ~233 K msg/s (criterion CI
  [232.77, 237.47] K elem/s). Criterion's change
  detector reports "No change in performance detected"
  vs the pre-migration baseline. Confirms the
  lifecycle API doesn't add overhead.
- `goalzc 0.5` (unpinned): ~229 K msg/s.
- `goalzc 0.5 --pin 0,1`: ~284 K msg/s.

### CLI breaking change in 0.6.0-2

`goalzc`'s `--warmup` / `--ticks` / `--decimals` flags
are gone. Existing scripts using them will fail at clap
parsing. Documented in this section; recipients can
re-derive throughput by simply running for longer (no
explicit warmup needed in a smoke generator).

  returns `()`. Per-window deltas would require atomics
  or signal-and-reply; both rejected as not worth the
  complexity at the user's KIS preference.
- **`spawn_and_seed` lives on `RuntimeZC`**, not as a
  free function, because it uses `self.pool` and is the
  natural place for the shared logic. Generic over `R`
  so both probe-free and probed actor loops share it.
- **Pool sizing in tests**: the lifecycle tests use the
  `Pool::new(., 4)` ping-pong-steady-state recommendation
  from the `0.5.0` chores (4 buffers covers 2 in flight
  + 2 headroom). One initial test draft used the
  capacity-2 pool from a non-running test and hit
  `NoMsgs` under traffic; standardized on 4.

### What's not in 0.6.0-1

- `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` still call the legacy
  `run_probed` / `run` paths. `0.6.0-2` migrates them.
- Legacy `RuntimeZC::run` / `run_probed` /
  `actor_loop_probed` / the `ClearProbe` signal path stay
  untouched for now. `0.6.0-3` removes them.

## Migrate goalzc bin and goalzc-crit bench to lifecycle (0.6.0-2)

`goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` move off the legacy all-in-one
`RuntimeZC::run_probed` / `run` to the lifecycle API
(`startup` / `Handle::run` / `Handle::stop`). `goalzc`
loses its band-table report (no probing in the runtime;
re-enters post-`0.6.0`) and becomes a smoke generator
that prints msg/s. `goalzc-crit` keeps its
cycle-per-sample shape, just through the new API.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.6.0-1` → `0.6.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`:
  - CLI: drop `--warmup` (`-w`), `--ticks` (`-t`),
    `--decimals` (`-d`). Keep `duration_s` (positional),
    `--size` (`-s`), `--pin` (`-p`).
  - Drop the apparatus calibration call and the
    per-actor `tprobe` band-table report.
  - Use `rt.startup` → `handle.run(duration)` →
    `handle.stop` to drive the actors.
  - Output: version banner, throughput line
    (`goalzc: <count> messages in <Ds> (<R> M msg/s,
    <N> actors, size=<S> B)`), and `pinning:` line if
    `--pin` was set. No `apparatus:` line, no band
    table.
  - Imports trimmed: `tprobe::{fmt_commas, pin}` only
    (was `tprobe::{self as perf, fmt_commas, pin,
    ticks}` plus probe-side report calls).
  - `n_actors = 2` hardcoded since the bin always
    creates two actors (assertion already pins
    `(a_id, b_id) == (0, 1)`).
- `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`:

    `window`. The lifecycle API has no mid-flight count
    read (counts only at `stop`), so a separate warmup
    phase would inflate the average; combining keeps
    the per-msg math honest.
  - Module docstring rewritten around the lifecycle
    API and the smoke-bench scope.
- `notes/todo.md`: mark `0.6.0-2` done.
- `notes/chores-03.md`: this section.
